_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the significant contributions of individuals and groups — including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and migrant communities — to Australian society.

_source.w: We are learning about the significant contributions of individuals and groups — including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and migrant communities — to Australian society.
y: Recognising diverse contributions helps us see that many different peoples have built modern Australia.
Celebrating contributions from all backgrounds challenges narrow views of Australian identity.
These stories inspire us to think about how we can contribute to our own communities.
eg: Researching an Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander leader who made significant contributions to Australian society.
Investigating how a migrant community contributed to the cultural life of a city or town.
Learning about women who fought for equal rights and changed Australian society.
Exploring the contributions of individuals in science, sport, the arts, or community leadership.
Discussing how diverse perspectives have strengthened Australia's democracy.
Creating a class display celebrating the contributions of different groups to Australian society.
